column1: #Text=It took years and years of pining and angst and overthinking, but the long game he's been playing finally paid off.
#Text=Their weekly lunch catch-up is now a weekly lunch
#Text=, and he could not be happier.
#Text="Clark, you are objectively the most beautiful man on our corner of the galaxy, so you really don't need it, but you do not have game.

column1: #Text=, Clark thinks, feeling the metaphorical bubble popping and deflating around him.
#Text="Hello, you've reached Bruce." he answers, pitching his voice to an upwards tilt.
#Text="You have to tell me, who is this again?
#Text="This call is so unusual and unexpected that I had to make sure.

column1: #Text="What a bizarre thing to say, I can order for myself."
#Text=It's quiet at the other end of the phone, then a sigh.
#Text=Clark replies, the tightness in his voice easing.
#Text="It's not your fault, he keeps things to himself, but he has his reasons."
#Text=A low chuckle from the other line, and
#Text="I really would love to grab a drink with you.

column1: #Text=Both Hal and Clark laugh at that.
#Text=He has no idea how much of all of this is specifically about him.
#Text="I don't know what Clark is thinking, but the three of us can't be seen together in public, it's too suspicious."
#Text="You just invited me to join you last week!"
#Text="I was planning to invite you both to the Manor.
